_comment: 50 synthetic portfolios graded from near-perfect to disaster. Failure modes are engineered via structure (sectors/weights), entry_price (entry ABOVE current = unrealized loss; entry FAR BELOW current + dominant share count = winner drift), and ticker choice (small caps / high-beta names). All tickers are real and were valid on Yahoo Finance as of May 2026; current price, sector, volatility and beta are fetched live at runtime, so exact weights/flags shift with the market. Prices in entry_price reflect ~May 2026 intuition.

portfolios.name: 01 Index Three-Fund
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. Classic VTI + VXUS + VOO + SCHD core. Tests index-fund detection and a maximally diversified baseline.

portfolios.name: 02 Diversified Blue Chips
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. One large-cap leader per sector, ~10% each across 10 sectors, all modest gainers. Should produce near 'portfolio healthy'.

portfolios.name: 03 Core + Satellite
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. ~45% VOO core with single-stock satellites spread across sectors, all small gains.

portfolios.name: 04 Dividend Diversified
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. Defensive dividend payers spread across sectors; low beta, modest gains.

portfolios.name: 05 Eight-Sector Spread
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. Eight single names, one per sector, ~12.5% each. Clean diversification, no index.

portfolios.name: 06 Global Balanced
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. US + international index tilt plus a couple quality single names.

portfolios.name: 07 Balanced Growth & Value
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. Growth and value names balanced across sectors with moderate weights.

portfolios.name: 08 Lazy Two-Fund
portfolios.description: NEAR-PERFECT. Total-market + international, dividend sleeve. The textbook set-and-forget.

portfolios.name: 09 Slightly Tech-Heavy
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. Diversified but tech sits a touch high (~30-35%). Otherwise balanced.

portfolios.name: 10 One Small Loser
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. Well diversified, but PFE is down ~40% from a high entry. Single moderate unrealized loss.

portfolios.name: 11 Mild Single-Stock Tilt
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. AAPL ~25% of the book — noticeable but below the concentration flag.

portfolios.name: 12 Modest Winner
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. NVDA appreciated from a low entry to ~28% — a winner just shy of the drift/concentration trigger.

portfolios.name: 13 Healthcare Lean
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. Overweight healthcare (~35%) but otherwise spread; tests sector-tilt detection at the boundary.

portfolios.name: 14 Thin Diversification
portfolios.description: GOOD-ish. Quality names but only three sectors represented — diversification is thinner than ideal.

portfolios.name: 15 Slight Beta Tilt
portfolios.description: GOOD, minor flaw. Mostly steady, but a couple of higher-beta growth names nudge portfolio beta up.

portfolios.name: 16 Tiny Dead-Weight Tail
portfolios.description: GOOD with a wart. Solid core plus two sub-1% odd-lot positions that read as dead weight.

portfolios.name: 17 Tech Overweight
portfolios.description: MODERATE. ~55% Technology across several chip/software names. Clear sector over-concentration.

portfolios.name: 18 Single-Stock Heavy
portfolios.description: MODERATE. NVDA ~40% of the portfolio — single-stock concentration above the trigger.

portfolios.name: 19 Two Losers
portfolios.description: MODERATE. Diversified but two positions (INTC, NKE) are down 40-55% from high entries.

portfolios.name: 20 Moderate Winner Drift
portfolios.description: MODERATE. AAPL bought cheap years ago, now ~38% with a drift multiple over 2x — winner drift + concentration.

portfolios.name: 21 Volatility Creep
portfolios.description: MODERATE. A steady core diluted with several higher-volatility names (TSLA, COIN, PLTR).

portfolios.name: 22 High-Beta Trio
portfolios.description: MODERATE-to-HIGH. Concentrated in high-beta megacap/growth (NVDA, TSLA, AMD) — elevated portfolio beta.

portfolios.name: 23 Small-Cap Dabbler
portfolios.description: MODERATE. Mostly large-cap core, but ~20% sprinkled into volatile small caps (SOFI, RUN, SOUN).

portfolios.name: 24 Three-Sector Gap
portfolios.description: MODERATE. All capital in tech, financials and energy — entire defensive/healthcare/utility side missing.

portfolios.name: 25 Energy Heavy
portfolios.description: MODERATE. ~45% energy across XOM/CVX/COP/SLB — strong sector bet.

portfolios.name: 26 Concentration + Loser
portfolios.description: MODERATE. NVDA ~38% concentration AND a deep BA loser (down ~35%) at the same time.

portfolios.name: 27 Drift + Separate Loser
portfolios.description: MODERATE. A drifted MSFT winner (low entry, dominant) plus an unrelated DIS loser down ~40%.

portfolios.name: 28 Dividend Trap
portfolios.description: MODERATE. Reaching for yield in beaten-down names (PFE, VZ, INTC) — value tilt with embedded losses.

portfolios.name: 29 All Tech
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. 100% Technology (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA/AVGO/ORCL) — maximal sector over-concentration.

portfolios.name: 30 Meme Basket
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Meme/retail favorites (GME, AMC, SOFI, HOOD) — high volatility, weak fundamentals, no diversification.

portfolios.name: 31 EV / Clean-Energy Wreck
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. EV and clean-energy small caps (PLUG, CHPT, RIVN, LCID) all bought far higher — deep losses + high vol.

portfolios.name: 32 Quantum Hype
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Pure quantum-computing speculation (IONQ, RGTI, QUBT) — extreme volatility, tiny revenue.

portfolios.name: 33 Crypto Miners
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Crypto-correlated names (MARA, RIOT, CLSK, COIN, MSTR) — sky-high beta and volatility.

portfolios.name: 34 Single Stock 60%
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. TSLA is ~60% of the portfolio — critical single-stock concentration in a high-beta name.

portfolios.name: 35 Extreme Winner Drift
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. AAPL bought at ~$20 long ago now dominates at ~75% — extreme winner drift + concentration.

portfolios.name: 36 Deep Losers Club
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Five positions all down 45-70% from entry (BA, INTC, PFE, NKE, DIS) — broad unrealized losses.

portfolios.name: 37 Small-Cap Junk Drawer
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Entirely speculative small caps (FUBO, BBAI, OPEN, UEC, HL, CDE) — illiquid, volatile, no large-cap anchor.

portfolios.name: 38 Two-Position Portfolio
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Only two holdings (AAPL + NVDA) — no diversification, ~50/50 concentration in one sector.

portfolios.name: 39 Energy All-In
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. ~80% energy (XOM/CVX/COP/SLB/EOG) — extreme single-sector bet on commodities.

portfolios.name: 40 Tech + Crypto Combo
portfolios.description: MESSED UP. Concentrated megacap tech stacked with speculative crypto exposure (MSTR, COIN) — correlated risk.

portfolios.name: 41 Single Small-Cap, 100% Loser
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Everything in one beaten-down small cap (PLUG) bought near the top — 100% concentration in a -70% position.

portfolios.name: 42 Speculative Inferno
portfolios.description: DISASTER. All high-vol small caps (SPCE, FCEL, BBAI, FUBO, SOUN), every one underwater. No diversification, no quality.

portfolios.name: 43 Winner Drift + Junk Losers
portfolios.description: DISASTER. One mega-winner-drift position (NVDA from $15) plus a tail of small-cap losers — concentration AND losses.

portfolios.name: 44 Beaten-Down Penny Disaster
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Sub-$7 names (OPEN, PLUG, CHPT, FUBO, AMC) all bought multiples higher — deep losses across low-priced, volatile tickers.

portfolios.name: 45 70% Concentrated Loser
portfolios.description: DISASTER. ~70% of the book in a single name (BA) that's also down ~35% — concentration and a deep loss in the same position.

portfolios.name: 46 All-In Quantum Micro
portfolios.description: DISASTER. 100% in quantum micro caps (RGTI, QUBT, IONQ) bought at the peak — maximal volatility, single-theme, underwater.

portfolios.name: 47 Meme + Crypto Casino
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Meme stocks plus crypto proxies (GME, AMC, MARA, MSTR, DJT) — pure speculation, extreme beta, no fundamentals.

portfolios.name: 48 Everything Wrong
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Hits every failure mode at once: a drifted concentrated winner (NVDA from $12), a deep loser (INTC -55%), a small-cap junk position, and a high-beta crypto name.

portfolios.name: 49 Leverage Lover
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Daily-leveraged ETFs (TQQQ, SOXL) plus a leveraged single-name bet — catastrophic volatility and decay risk.

portfolios.name: 50 The Gambler
portfolios.description: DISASTER. Two speculative names only (RIVN + COIN), both bought far higher — no diversification, deep losses, maximal caution.
